pop.description: Commercial popular music with catchy melodies, polished production, and broad mainstream appeal.

rock.description: Guitar-driven music ranging from classic rock to alternative. Emphasis on live instruments and raw energy.

lofi.description: Low-fidelity hip-hop influenced instrumental music. Warm, slightly imperfect sound ideal for studying or relaxing.

jazz.description: Improvisational music rooted in blues and swing. Ranges from mellow background jazz to energetic bebop.

edm.description: Electronic Dance Music. Synthesizer-driven, built for clubs and high-energy environments.

hip-hop.description: Urban music built around beats, rhythm, and rap vocals. Covers introspective to aggressive styles.

r&b.description: Rhythm and blues. Soulful, melodic, and often romantic. Blends pop structure with gospel and funk roots.

classical.description: Orchestral and composed Western music. Wide range from quiet chamber pieces to dramatic symphonies.

country.description: American roots music blending folk, blues, and pop. Storytelling lyrics, acoustic and electric guitar.

metal.description: High-distortion guitar music with aggressive drumming. Emphasizes intensity, power, and technical precision.

funk.description: Groove-oriented music emphasizing rhythm, bass, and syncopation. Danceable and upbeat.

folk.description: Acoustic, storytelling music rooted in traditional and roots music. Often introspective and organic.

ambient.description: Atmospheric, texture-focused music designed to set a mood rather than drive a beat. Very low energy.

synthwave.description: Retro-futuristic electronic music inspired by 1980s film scores and video games. Moody and atmospheric.

indie pop.description: Independent alternative pop music. Melodic with a more experimental or lo-fi edge than mainstream pop.

reggae.description: Jamaican music with a distinctive offbeat rhythm. Relaxed tempo, often with social themes.

soul.description: Emotionally expressive music descended from gospel and R&B. Emphasis on vocals and feeling.

electronic.description: Broad category of synthesizer-based music spanning dance, ambient, and experimental styles.

indie.description: Independent music produced outside major labels. Often guitar-based with an alternative sensibility.

classical guitar.description: Solo or small ensemble acoustic guitar compositions in the classical tradition.

blues.description: American roots music based on call-and-response vocals and guitar. Emotional and expressive.
